briefdescription.para: GLES version 2.0.
GLES version 3.0.
Enumeration for GLES verion.

detaileddescription.para: This Enumeration is used the GLES version for EGL configuration. If the device can not support GLES version 3.0 over, the version will be chosen with GLES version 2.0

briefdescription.para: continuous mode
on demand by application
Enumeration for rendering mode.

detaileddescription.para: This Enumeration is used to choose the rendering mode. It has two options. One of them is continuous mode. It is rendered continuously. The other is on demand mode. It is rendered by application.

briefdescription.para: GlWindow Key Event signal type.
GlWindow Touch Event signal type.
GlWindow Focus signal type.
GlWindow resize signal type.
GlWindow visibility change signal type.
Creates an initialized handle to a new GlWindow.

simplesect.para: A new GlWindow
This creates an extra GlWindow in addition to the default main GlWindow

memberdef.argsstring: (PositionSize positionSize, const std::string &name, const std::string &className, bool isTransparent=false)
memberdef.name: New

briefdescription.para: Creates an initialized handle to a new GlWindow.

parameterdescription.para: The position and size of the GlWindow
The GlWindow title
The GlWindow class name
Whether GlWindow is transparent

simplesect.para: This creates an extra GlWindow in addition to the default main GlWindow
A new GlWindow

briefdescription.para: Creates an uninitialized handle.

detaileddescription.para: This can be initialized using Dali::Application::GetGlWindow() or Dali::GlWindow::New().

briefdescription.para: Destructor.

detaileddescription.para: This is non-virtual since derived Handle types must not contain data or virtual methods.

briefdescription.para: This copy constructor is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

parameterdescription.para: A reference to the copied handle

briefdescription.para: This assignment operator is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

parameterdescription.para: A reference to the copied handle

simplesect.para: A reference to this

briefdescription.para: Move constructor.

parameterdescription.para: A reference to the moved handle

briefdescription.para: Move assignment operator.

parameterdescription.para: A reference to the moved handle

simplesect.para: A reference to this handle

memberdef.argsstring: (bool depth, bool stencil, int msaa, GlesVersion version)
memberdef.name: SetGraphicsConfig

briefdescription.para: Sets graphics configuration for GlWindow.

parameterdescription.para: the flag of depth buffer. If true is set, 24bit depth buffer is enabled.
the flag of stencil. it true is set, 8bit stencil buffer is enabled.
the bit of msaa.
the GLES version

briefdescription.para: Raises GlWindow to the top of GlWindow stack.
Lowers GlWindow to the bottom of GlWindow stack.
Activates GlWindow to the top of GlWindow stack even it is iconified.
Shows the GlWindow if it is hidden.
Hides the GlWindow if it is showing.
Sets a position of the GlWindow.

parameterdescription.para: The new GlWindow position

briefdescription.para: Gets a position of the GlWindow.

simplesect.para: The position of the GlWindow

briefdescription.para: Gets the count of supported auxiliary hints of the window.

simplesect.para: The number of supported auxiliary hints.

The window auxiliary hint is the value which is used to decide which actions should be made available to the user by the window manager. If you want to set specific hint to your window, then you should check whether it exists in the supported auxiliary hints.

briefdescription.para: Gets the supported auxiliary hint string of the window.

parameterdescription.para: The index of the supported auxiliary hint lists

simplesect.para: The auxiliary hint string of the index.

The window auxiliary hint is the value which is used to decide which actions should be made available to the user by the window manager. If you want to set specific hint to your window, then you should check whether it exists in the supported auxiliary hints.

briefdescription.para: Creates an auxiliary hint of the window.

parameterdescription.para: The auxiliary hint string.
The value string.

simplesect.para: The ID of created auxiliary hint, or 0 on failure.

briefdescription.para: Removes an auxiliary hint of the window.

parameterdescription.para: The ID of the auxiliary hint.

simplesect.para: True if no error occurred, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Changes a value of the auxiliary hint.

parameterdescription.para: The auxiliary hint ID.
The value string to be set.

simplesect.para: True if no error occurred, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Gets a value of the auxiliary hint.

parameterdescription.para: The auxiliary hint ID.

simplesect.para: The string value of the auxiliary hint ID, or an empty string if none exists.

briefdescription.para: Gets a ID of the auxiliary hint string.

parameterdescription.para: The auxiliary hint string.

simplesect.para: The ID of the auxiliary hint string, or 0 if none exists.

briefdescription.para: Sets a region to accept input events.

parameterdescription.para: The region to accept input events.

briefdescription.para: Sets a transparent window's visual state to opaque.

detaileddescription.para: If a visual state of a transparent window is opaque, then the window manager could handle it as an opaque window when calculating visibility.

parameterdescription.para: Whether the window's visual state is opaque.

simplesect.para: This will have no effect on an opaque window. It doesn't change transparent window to opaque window but lets the window manager know the visual state of the window.

briefdescription.para: Returns whether a transparent window's visual state is opaque or not.

simplesect.para: True if the window's visual state is opaque, false otherwise.
The return value has no meaning on an opaque window.

briefdescription.para: Gets current rotation angle of the window.

simplesect.para: The current GlWindow rotation angle if previously set, or none

briefdescription.para: Sets available orientations of the window.

detaileddescription.para: This API is for setting several orientations one time.

parameterdescription.para: The available orientations list to add

briefdescription.para: Sets a preferred orientation.

parameterdescription.para: The preferred orientation

simplesect.para: angle is in the list of available orientation.
To unset the preferred orientation, angle should be set NO_ORIENTATION_PREFERENCE.

briefdescription.para: Registers a GL callback function for application.

detaileddescription.para:
initCallback
the callback function for application initialize
renderFrameCallback
the callback function to render for the frame.
terminateCallback
the callback function to clean-up application GL resource.
Function must be called on idle time
A initCallback of the following type should be used: voidintializeGL(); This callback will be called before renderFrame callback is called at once.
A renderFrameCallback of the following type should be used: intrenderFrameGL(); This callback's return value is not 0, the eglSwapBuffers() will be called.
A terminateCallback of the following type should be used: voidterminateGL(); This callback is called when GlWindow is deleted.

briefdescription.para: Renders once more even if GL render functions are not added to idler.

simplesect.para: Will not work if the window is hidden or GL render functions are added to idler

briefdescription.para: Sets rendering mode.

parameterdescription.para: the rendering mode for GlWindow

simplesect.para: The default Rendering mode is continuous. If OnDemand mode is set, it is rendered by RenderOnce()

briefdescription.para: Gets rendering mode.

simplesect.para: current rendering mode in this GlWindow
The default Rendering mode is continuous. If OnDemand mode is set, it is rendered by RenderOnce()

briefdescription.para: The user should connect to this signal to get a timing when GlWindow gains focus or loses focus.

detaileddescription.para: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(GlWindowGlWindow,boolfocusIn); The parameter is true if GlWindow gains focus, otherwise false. and GlWindow means this signal was called from what GlWindow

simplesect.para: The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when the GlWindow is resized.

detaileddescription.para: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(GlWindowGlWindow,intwidth,intheight); The parameters are the resized width and height. and GlWindow means this signal was called from what GlWindow

simplesect.para: The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when key event is received.

detaileddescription.para: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(constKeyEvent&event);

simplesect.para: The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when the screen is touched and when the touch ends (i.e. the down & up touch events only).

detaileddescription.para: If there are multiple touch points, then this will be emitted when the first touch occurs and then when the last finger is lifted. An interrupted event will also be emitted (if it occurs). A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(constTouchEvent&event);

simplesect.para: The touch signal to connect to
Motion events are not emitted.

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when the window is shown or hidden.

detaileddescription.para: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(Windowwindow,boolvisible);

simplesect.para: The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: The GlWindow class is to draw with native GLES.

detaileddescription.para: This class is the special window. It is for native GLES application. So, some special funtions and type are supported. In addition, basic window's functions are supported, too.
